title: Micromeria eugenioides
intro: Micromeria eugenioides is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae), commonly found in Mediterranean regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and its leaves have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and inflammation, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Micromeria eugenioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Micromeria eugenioides is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory complaints, as well as to aid digestion and relieve menstrual cramps.
taxonomy: Micromeria eugenioides is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to the Western Ghats of India. This plant is classified within the genus Micromeria, which is part of the mint family. Micromeria eugenioides is further classified as a member of the tribe Mentheae, one of the largest tribes in the Lamiaceae family. As a species of the plant family Lamiaceae, Micromeria eugenioides has a complex classification that is closely related to other plants in the mint family.

side_effects: Micromeria eugenioides, a plant native to the Mediterranean region, is known to have some potential side effects due to its high concentration of essential oils. The essential oils present in the plant, particularly thymol and carvacrol, can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues if ingested in large quantities. Ingesting the plant can also lead to interactions with certain medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications. Furthermore, the plant's essential oils may cause respiratory problems in individuals with asthma or other respiratory conditions.
